title: Crypto humor
author: AdHefty3288
selftext:
There's a well-known joke in the crypto world about how this value has evolved since its inception. A computer programmer in Florida wanted to buy pizza in 2010. And he persuaded someone to take Bitcoin rather than cash. He spent $40 in Bitcoin on two Papa John's pizzas. That sum is now worth almost $400 million. As a result, individuals use Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies as if they were actual money to purchase goods. They are, however, investing in it as if it were a stock. Crypto is not your usual investment. If you buy shares in a car firm, for example, you believe the company produces high-quality automobiles. You believe they will sell a lot this year. So you invest your money and receive a stake in the firm. When you buy a coin as an investment in Bitcoin or crypto, you're basically investing in the concept that the virtual currency itself is valuable and that others will continue to find it valuable. To be honest, I believe cryptocurrency has the potential to become real future money. Cryptocurrency is becoming a solution that many people in our society do not grasp, owing to significant benefits over fiat cash.  
Another indicator of cryptocurrency's success, in my perspective, is the rise of projects such as Bind.com. BIND is a next-generation crypto-fiat financial platform that will connect traditional banking with decentralized blockchain technology and make this solution available to a worldwide audience. It will have licensed traditional payment infrastructure as well as rapid payment processing all around the world. BIND used the opposite tactic of "finding" consumers by creating a straightforward end-to-end solution for company owners. Why? Many purchasers are now willing to use cryptocurrencies to pay for necessities such as food, clothing, and housing. Our future is here!
